campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth afford recreation programs. They also discussed extending a temporary rule that was put in place urgently, and moved forward with plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
City extending temporary ordinance 2326 (urgency measure)
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17
Final feasibility study approved for Highway 17 bicycle overcrossing

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to comment on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process to speed things up. The council plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new ordinance
First reading requirement waived to accelerate approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Ordinance moved to consent calendar for adoption

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to handle several administrative and community matters, including approving final reports on weed removal efforts and establishing new council procedures. The council also approved raising two flags to celebrate Pride Month and Juneteenth, discussed affordable housing policies, and received a presentation from the regional transit authority about transportation planning.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program across the city
New rules and procedures for how City Council conducts meetings
Policies for transferring affordable housing units to new owners
Plans to raise Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags at city facilities
Regional transit authority updates and housing development timeline

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with ongoing projects and services. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign contracts for construction work with QLM, Inc., professional services with Dudek, and various amendments to existing agreements. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance authority (CJPIA) to help manage the city's insurance costs.
keyTopics: Construction contract approval with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority (CJPIA)
Multiple amendments to existing service agreements
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how the city is supporting kids and teens through various programs and services. They discussed updates on youth programs like the Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative, checked on how the library system is doing with its facilities and customer service, and reviewed how well code enforcement is working across the city.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant programs
Children and Youth Services Master Plan progress and updates
Library facilities improvements and customer experience
Code Enforcement Annual Report findings
Youth Intervention Services Programs performance

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents could join the virtual meeting via Zoom or phone to listen and provide public comment on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Meeting conducted with hybrid in-person and online components

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about life in Sunnyvale
Approval of Human Relations Commission meeting minutes from March 12, 2026
Public hearings and general business items

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 8, 2026 to discuss several community programs including a food and budget initiative called Budget and Bites, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and job opportunities for young people through the city's Summer Jobs program. The council also approved meeting minutes from their March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any other local issues for three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program (food and financial assistance initiative)
Unmuted Tabs youth organization presentation
Summer Jobs program for young people
Public comment period for residents
Approval of previous meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online formats so residents can participate however works best for them. The city is making it easy for people to attend and share their opinions by offering Zoom access and phone dial-in options.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Public can join via Zoom or phone (1-669-900-6833)
Written comments can be submitted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page
Meeting accessible to remote participants throughout the session

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a local educational organization, and awarding a construction contract for a city project. The council also reviewed proposed updates to the city's housing and community development plan and discussed new work priorities suggested by individual council members.
keyTopics: Tax Board partnership with Franchise Tax Board and county
Fee waiver and donation request for Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract approval and project plans
Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New workplan priorities from council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed and approved gas utility plans for the upcoming fiscal year, including changes to how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service. The meeting covered financial forecasts, reserve funds, and new rate schedules for different types of gas customers (residential, commercial, and large commercial users).
keyTopics: Gas utility rates going up for residents and businesses in FY 2027
Financial forecast and reserve fund transfers for the gas utility
Rate schedule changes for three customer categories (residential, commercial, large commercial)
One-hour discussion period held during evening meeting
